product_name: Tap-Along Sounder Snare Drum with Double Sided Batter Head Toy Tambourine Drum for Kids, Musical toys for Children 12 Months and Up Realistic Look with 2 Drum Sticks 10X10X4

bullets: TOY SNARE DRUM: Tap your hand or drum sticks on the playing surface to make a sound. Allow your child to explore rhythm and beats with this fun musical instrument.
MODERATE VOLUME: This instrument is super fun and easy on the ear. The Double-sided batter Head helps keep the volume crisp and realistic. Great musical toy for kids 12 months and up
REALISTICALLY LOOKING MUSICAL SET: This tambourine toy looks like a real drum. Whether you are singing alone or playing with a band, this will help you stand out.
CREATIVE PLAY: The Sounder KsmToys Tap-along Tambourine stimulates speech and singing ability in toddlers and promotes the development of hand-eye coordination as well as sensory perception

MADE FOR CHILD HANDS: Sized for tiny hands to hold, tapping this tambourine to music will awaken a child's senses and get them on their feet to dance. Little musicians will have hours of enjoyable creative play. Made in EU Spain. 7X3X7"
content.keywords: Musical toys toddlers 1-3, kids instruments, baby musical instruments, toddler instruments, music toys, musical toys.
content.description: At KsmToys we have put so much effort into introducing this incredible new musical instrument line we are so proud of! Here's why you should encourage your child to play a musical instrument: Research has found that learning music facilitates learning other subjects and enhances skills children inevitably use in other areas. A study by E. Glenn Schellenberg at the University of Toronto at Mississauga, as published in a 2004 issue of Psychological Science, found an increase in the child's IQ. Research indicates the brain of a musician, even a young one, works differently than that of a nonmusician. Children involved in music have significantly more growth of neural activity than people not in music training. " When you're a musician, and you're playing an instrument, you have to be using more of your brain," says Dr. Eric Rasmussen of The Johns Hopkins University. Research has also found a causal link between music and spatial intelligence. Understanding music can help children visualize elements that should go together as they would do when solving a math problem. And most importantly, MUSIC JUST MAKES YOU HAPPY!

compliance.choking_hazard: WARNING: CHOKING HAZARD--Small parts. Not intended for children under 3 yrs
compliance.warranty_desc: 1 year agains all defects in manufacuring and or materials
